title: B.Sc. (Hons) in Mathematics with Specialization in Computational Mathematics
content: In addition to its core mathematical rigor, the B.Sc. Mathematics (Honours) program is a three year degree program which offers a specialized stream in Computational Mathematics, which equips students with the skills to solve complex mathematical problems using algorithmic and numerical methods. This stream includes courses such as Computational Mathematics, Advanced Numerical Techniques, Data Structures and Algorithms, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, and Blockchain Technologies, ensuring students are proficient in both theory and application. With hands-on exposure to programming languages like Python and tools like MATLAB or Scilab, students develop the capability to model, simulate, and analyze real-world systems. The integration of computational mathematics enhances problem-solving efficiency and prepares graduates for cutting-edge careers in data analytics, software development, quantitative finance, and scientific research. 10+2 with Science, including Mathematics as one of the subjects, with a minimum aggregate of 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list 01 This program trains students in computational tools like MATLAB for solving optimization and simulation problems, modeling real-world systems in engineering/science, and translating numerical results into practical applications with precision and efficiency. 01 02 03 Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern scientific and IT tools including prediction and modelling to complex scientific activities with an understanding of the limitations. 04 05 06 07 08 01 Graduates will thrive as computational mathematicians or software developers in tech firms, optimizing algorithms for simulations in engineering or finance. 02 Graduates will design numerical solutions for real-time problems using tools like MATLAB or Julia, achieving efficiency gains in computational tasks. 03 Graduates will integrate math modeling into industry applications, such as optimization in logistics, with proven project outcomes. 04 Graduates will advance in professional development, pursuing certifications in computational fields and contributing to cross-disciplinary teams. 01 Mathematical Foundations: Understanding core concepts, principles, and techniques in computing and related mathematical foundations, enabling effective problem-solving, algorithm development, and application of computing solutions across diverse domains. 02 Computational Techniques: Apply computational methods and numerical algorithms using tools like Scilab, Python, R etc. to solve mathematical problems that arise in science, engineering, and technology. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list
